message:
test(raptorq): whitespace-tolerant env parsing, factor-one elimination edge cases, and governance fallback coverage

Three testing improvements across the RaptorQ subsystem:

GF256 (gf256.rs):
- parse_usize_env() now trims whitespace before parsing, matching how
  real environments behave (trailing newlines from shell, leading spaces
  from config files). Without this, " 12345 " would silently fall back
  to defaults.
- New parametric test exercises all six numeric env override keys with
  various whitespace combinations (\n, \t, spaces) and verifies that
  the trimmed value is applied, canonical provenance is scrubbed, and
  the manual-override metadata chain is intact.
- New test: max_lane_ratio_numeric_override_clamps_zero_to_one verifies
  that a zero max_lane_ratio is clamped to 1 (division-by-zero guard).

Linear algebra (linalg.rs):
- Two new eliminate_row tests for the factor=ONE fast path where the
  elimination is a pure XOR (no GF(256) multiply). One test targets a
  row after the pivot, the other before it. Both verify that RHS vectors
  are correctly resized to the wider operand before XOR and that the
  coefficient tail is properly zeroed.

Test log schema (test_log_schema.rs):
- New test iterates all entries in G7_RUNTIME_FALLBACK_REASONS and
  validates that each is accepted by validate_unit_log_json when used
  as the deterministic_fallback_trigger.reason. This prevents regressions
  where adding a new runtime fallback reason to the governance contract
  breaks schema validation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
